hero.title: RobôCIn
hero.subtitle: A research and development team from the Federal University of Pernambuco, Brazil, specializing in robotics and artificial intelligence.

partners.title: Partnerships
partners.description: Connect your company to RobôCIn's innovation and expertise to develop intelligent solutions that shape the future of technology.
partners.caption: Who has already trusted our work:

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: The latest news, research updates, and insights from the RobôCIn team.

publications.title: Publications
publications.subtitle: The academic contributions from the RobôCIn team to the fields of robotics and AI.

categoriesPage.title: Competition Categories
categoriesPage.description: RobôCIn competes across four distinct robotics leagues, each presenting unique engineering and AI challenges. Explore each category to learn about our robots, approach, and competition history.

ssl.name: Small Size League (SSL)
ssl.description: The SSL is one of the most technically demanding RoboCup leagues. Teams of up to six omni-directional robots compete on a shared field, relying on centralized AI and high-speed computer vision to outmaneuver opponents in real time.
bullets: Robots are constrained to 180 mm diameter and operate at speeds exceeding 3 m/s
Centralized strategy software processes overhead camera feeds at 60 fps
Machine learning models drive opponent prediction and dynamic play selection

sim2d.name: 2D Simulation
sim2d.description: In the 2D Simulation league, multi-agent AI systems coach virtual soccer teams inside a physics simulator. With no hardware constraints, teams push the limits of strategy, coordination, and learning algorithms.
bullets: Fully software-based: 11 autonomous agents vs. 11 in a shared 2D environment
Focus areas include multi-agent reinforcement learning and emergent team tactics
Competitions run on identical hardware, making algorithmic quality the sole differentiator

drone.name: Drone
drone.description: The Drone category challenges teams to develop autonomous unmanned aerial vehicles capable of navigation, object detection, and interaction tasks in complex indoor environments without any external computing.
bullets: Onboard processing only — no external computing allowed during flight
Tasks include obstacle avoidance, target recognition, and precision landing
Tight integration of computer vision, control theory, and embedded software

humanoid.name: Humanoid
humanoid.description: Humanoid robots must walk, balance, and play soccer on a small field, showcasing advances in bipedal locomotion, real-time vision, and autonomous decision-making under physical constraints.
bullets: Full bipedal locomotion with dynamic balance and fall recovery
Onboard vision pipeline for ball and goal detection without external sensors
Motion planning adapts in real time to field conditions and game state

history.title: Our Journey
history.subtitle: Over two decades building the future of Brazilian robotics.

milestones.title: Foundation
milestones.description: RobôCIn is founded at CIn-UFPE by students passionate about robotics and artificial intelligence.

milestones.title: First RoboCup
milestones.description: The team participates for the first time in RoboCup, the world's largest robotics competition, marking the start of a long international journey.

milestones.title: Expansion of Fronts
milestones.description: New labs and projects broaden the group's work in computer vision, embedded systems, and machine learning.

milestones.title: World Titles
milestones.description: RobôCIn wins titles in the SSL and VSS categories at RoboCup, establishing Brazil among the world's top robotics powers.

milestones.title: 25 Years of Innovation
milestones.description: With over 200 members and a presence at competitions across five continents, the group celebrates 25 years of advancing science and technology.
